_comment: Curated MCP catalog. Each entry describes a popular Model Context Protocol server that Axocoatl users can install in one click. Updated manually — bump the version when entries change so the dashboard can detect a refresh. Source of truth: https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers (Anthropic) plus widely-used third-party servers. `args_template` and `env_template` use {{PLACEHOLDER}} markers; the install modal collects values and substitutes them before writing to the running mcp_servers list.

servers.name: Filesystem
servers.description: Read, write, list, and edit files within explicitly allowlisted directories. Safer than giving an agent shell access — every path is constrained to roots you pre-declare.

servers.name: Brave Search
servers.description: Web search via Brave's API. A free tier covers ~2k queries/month — useful when you want real-time information without Tavily's pricing.

servers.name: GitHub
servers.description: Read repos, browse issues + PRs, search code, create comments, manage branches. Personal access token defines scope.

requires.label: GitHub personal access token
requires.help: github.com → Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens. Grant only the repos + scopes you want the agent to see.

servers.name: Postgres
servers.description: Read-only SQL access to a Postgres database. The agent can introspect schema and run SELECTs but never mutates.

servers.name: SQLite
servers.description: Read and write a local SQLite database. Tools: schema introspection, parameterized queries, table-create. Great for agent-managed scratch databases.

servers.name: Knowledge Graph Memory
servers.description: Persistent agent memory in a local knowledge graph. Tools to create entities, link relations, query — the LLM builds its own structured recall on top of Axocoatl's tier-4 semantic memory.

servers.name: Sequential Thinking
servers.description: A scratch-pad tool for the model to lay out multi-step reasoning before acting. Particularly useful for non-reasoning models that benefit from explicit chain-of-thought.

servers.name: Time
servers.description: Current time and timezone conversion. Sounds small; it's the difference between an agent guessing what 'tomorrow' means and being right.

servers.name: Fetch
servers.description: HTTP GET (and turn HTML into clean markdown). The minimal web-browsing primitive — pairs well with a search server.

servers.name: Puppeteer
servers.description: Real browser automation — navigate pages, click, screenshot, fill forms. Heavier than Fetch; use when the target site needs JavaScript.

servers.name: Google Maps
servers.description: Geocoding, places search, directions, distance matrix. Requires a Google Maps API key with the Places + Directions APIs enabled.

servers.name: Slack
servers.description: Read channels, post messages, list users. Scope is whatever your Slack bot token allows — pin it down on creation.

servers.name: Google Drive
servers.description: Search and read Google Drive files. Read-only — agents can't accidentally delete things. OAuth flow runs once at first connect.

servers.name: Git
servers.description: Local git operations on an allowlisted repo — log, diff, blame, status. Read-only by default; explicit args enable commit/push.

servers.name: Everything (reference server)
servers.description: Anthropic's reference implementation showcasing every MCP feature — resources, tools, prompts, sampling. Useful for testing your wiring, not for production.
